label: Metaconceptual Art origin provenance
description: Machine-readable origin chronology for Metaconceptual Art. This file distinguishes the studio formation, movement origin, early artwork evidence, and later public linked-data publication so the project is not framed as a retrospective 2026 invention.

events.label: Sun & Rain Works established
events.description: Sun & Rain Works was established as the art business and studio context for the later Metaconceptual Art movement.

events.label: Metaconceptual Art movement created
events.description: Metaconceptual Art is asserted by Sun & Rain Works as a movement created in 2007, before the 2026 linked-data publication layer.

events.label: The Auction as Artwork
events.description: In March 2009, in connection with the Sotheby's Photographs auction (sale N08533, New York), Sun & Rain Works consigned an Ansel Adams photograph, 'San Francisco from Twin Peaks' (lot 16), and designates its engagement with that auction as a Metaconceptual Art work, after the logic of the readymade. The auction was carried out by Sotheby's; the artistic act is Sun & Rain Works' designation, asserted by the artist and made explicit over time. This is not a publicly witnessed live-creation event and carries no adjudicated priority claim; the catalogue does not name Sun & Rain Works. Two associated works accompany the gesture: www.wtfisart.com and WTF is Ansel Adams. Supporting records (2008 Sotheby's correspondence, the public lot, receipts, photographs) are held by the studio; selected evidence can be promoted to public as redaction and rights review are completed.

events.label: www.wtfisart.com (website)
events.description: A website built and owned by Sun & Rain Works around March 2009, associated by the studio with the conceptual work The Auction as Artwork. Reported documentation includes domain-registration and hosting receipts and site metadata evidencing the website's existence and date; the archive is held by the studio. It is not recorded as having been authored live in the auction room.

events.label: WTF is Ansel Adams (conceptual designation)
events.description: A conceptual work that Sun & Rain Works designates around its consignment of the Ansel Adams photograph (lot 16) to Sotheby's Photographs, March 2009. It is an assertion by the artist, made explicit over time, and is not recorded as a publicly witnessed performance. Supporting records are held by the studio.

events.label: Public linked-data publication
events.description: The 2026 web publication makes the movement, profile, selected works, and records machine-readable as Linked Art, IIIF, JSON-LD, SHACL, VoID, and DCAT.

comparisonPolicy.label: Comparison and priority policy
comparisonPolicy.description: Earlier studio evidence may establish priority for a documented Metaconceptual Art structure without asserting direct influence on later artists or events unless evidence of contact, access, or derivation is separately published.
comparisonPolicy.recommendedClaim: Documented prior evidence for a related metaconceptual structure.
comparisonPolicy.avoidClaim: Direct influence, copying, or misattribution without separate evidence.
